declared.title: State of Internet Freedom in Africa 2025: Navigating the Implications of AI on Digital Democracy in Uganda
declared.selection_rationale: Single-country companion report to the continent-wide 2025 SIFA report already in this corpus (cipesa-africa-2025-sifa-ai) -- not a duplicate of it. Confirmed by fetching the PDF directly: full structured report (Executive Summary, Country Context, Research Methodology, Study Findings, Discussion, Conclusion/ Recommendations), written by Edrine Wanyama, published September 2025. Coverage-contribution value: the continent report's Uganda material is necessarily compressed across many countries; this gives Uganda-specific depth on AI's civic-space risks (covered at the section level: value of AI to civic space, risks to digital rights, state of AI regulation, human-rights-centred governance recommendations) that the continent edition can't provide at the same depth. Kenya and Ethiopia have equivalent country-specific 2025 SIFA AI reports in the same catalog series, confirmed to exist but not yet individually verified -- candidates for a follow-up pass, not added this round.
